hero.title:
AI inference,
routed openly
hero.sub: Decentralized inference gateway powered by community GPUs. Transparent billing, Lightning payments, 100% open source.
feat.title: Why RouteCat?
feat.sub: Everything a closed gateway does, but you can read every line of code.
feat.open.p: Gateway, billing, routing — all public on GitHub. Audit the code that handles your money.
feat.ln.p: Providers earn sats automatically. Users pay per token. No accounts, no credit cards, no middlemen.
feat.route.p: Requests are routed to the best available node by model, VRAM, queue depth, and region.
feat.bill.p: Per-token pricing, 5% flat fee, every job logged in SQLite. No hidden charges.
feat.api.p: Drop-in replacement for the OpenAI API. Change one line — your base URL — and it works.
feat.priv.p: No accounts, no emails, no tracking. We don't log your prompts or responses. Provider nodes never know who you are. Your API key is your only identity.
feat.comm.p: Anyone with a GPU can join as a provider. Earn Bitcoin from idle compute. No lock-in.
priv.title: Your prompts are yours
priv.body: RouteCat acts as a privacy proxy between you and the inference nodes. We don't store, log, or inspect your prompts or responses. Provider nodes never see your identity — they only receive the raw inference request. No emails, no sign-ups, no cookies. Your API key is the only link to your account, and it's generated locally with no personal data attached.
pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.sub: Pay per token. Providers keep 95%, gateway takes 5%.
pricing.note: 10 free playground requests per day. Top up with Lightning for API access.
calc.title: What can you do with your sats?
calc.note: Estimate based on output tokens. Actual usage depends on prompt length and response complexity.
api.title: API
api.sub: OpenAI-compatible. Works with any SDK or curl.
account.title: Your Account
account.sub: Get an API key. Top up with Lightning. Check your balance.
account.nokey.desc: Create a free API key to try the playground (10 requests/day), or enter an existing key.
account.nokey.name: App name (optional)
account.login.label: Already have a key?
account.login.err.format: Invalid key format — must start with rc_
account.key.desc: This key will not be shown again. If you lose it, you lose access to any funds deposited. Copy it and store it safely.
account.topup.label: Top up:
account.topup.range: Amount must be between 10 and 100,000 sats
pg.title: Playground
pg.sub: Try it live. No sign-up needed for a quick test.
pg.default: Tell me a short story about an extremely lazy cat who accidentally becomes the world's best message router.
pg.nokey: Create an API key in the Account section above first.
prov.title: Run a node
prov.sub: Got a GPU? Earn Bitcoin by serving inference.
prov.quickstart.text:
1. Install Ollama and pull a model
2. Install Owlrun (our fork with multi-language dashboard)
3. Edit ~/.owlrun/owlrun.conf
4. Restart — start earning sats
faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: How does RouteCat protect my privacy?
faq.a1: RouteCat acts as a proxy between you and the inference nodes. Your prompts and responses are forwarded in real time and never stored. Provider nodes only see the raw model request — they don't know who you are. No emails, no cookies, no tracking. Your API key is your only identity, and it carries no personal information.
faq.q2: How can I verify the gateway is honest?
faq.a2: Three ways: (1) The code is open source — read it yourself. (2) The /v1/stats endpoint shows the exact git commit hash of the running binary — you can rebuild from source and compare. (3) The /v1/audit endpoint shows all completed jobs with token counts, earnings, and fees — no hidden charges.
faq.a3: Providers set a Lightning address and a payout threshold (in sats). When their earned balance exceeds the threshold, the gateway automatically sends a Lightning payment. Providers keep 95% of every job — the gateway takes a flat 5% fee. All payouts are verifiable on-chain via the payment hash.
faq.q4: How do I pay for API access?
faq.a4: Create an API key (free, 10 playground requests/day). For API access, top up your balance by paying a Lightning invoice — any amount from 10 to 100,000 sats. Your balance is deducted per-token based on the model's pricing. No subscriptions, no minimum commitments.
faq.q5: What happens if I lose my API key?
faq.a5: Your API key is shown only once when you create it. If you lose it, you lose access to any remaining balance. There is no recovery mechanism — this is by design, as we don't collect any personal data to verify your identity. Always save your key securely.
faq.q6: Is RouteCat compatible with OpenAI SDKs?
faq.a6: Yes. Set base_url="https://route.cat/v1" and your API key in any OpenAI-compatible client (Python, Node.js, curl). Streaming, chat completions, and model listing all work the same way.
footer.oss: RouteCat is open source under MIT license.
docs.title: RouteCat Docs
docs.intro.title: RouteCat API Documentation
docs.intro.subtitle: The open-source AI inference gateway. Access any model, pay per token with Bitcoin Lightning, powered by community GPU providers.
docs.intro.p1: RouteCat is an open-source gateway that routes AI inference requests to a decentralized network of GPU providers running Owlrun nodes. It exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, so any existing client or library works out of the box.
docs.intro.p2: Providers earn Bitcoin for serving requests. Users pay per token with Lightning Network micropayments — no subscriptions, no vendor lock-in.
docs.intro.p3: All endpoints are relative to this base URL. The API follows the OpenAI specification where applicable, so you can use the official openai Python/JS libraries by changing the base_url.
docs.quickstart.title: Quick Start
docs.quickstart.desc: Get up and running in three steps.
docs.quickstart.step1_desc: Register for a free key. You get 10 free requests per day with no payment required.
docs.quickstart.step2_desc: Use your API key to call the chat completions endpoint.
docs.quickstart.step3_desc: You get back a standard OpenAI-compatible response.
docs.quickstart.tip_body: You can use the official openai Python library by setting base_url="https://route.cat/v1" and your RouteCat API key.
docs.auth.title: Authentication
docs.auth.p1: All authenticated requests require a valid API key sent via the Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.
docs.auth.key_format_desc: API keys always start with the rc_ prefix followed by a random alphanumeric string. Keep your key secret — anyone with it can make requests on your behalf.
docs.auth.rate_limit_warn_body: All API keys are rate-limited to 60 requests per minute. Exceeding this limit returns a 429 status code. Free-tier keys are additionally capped at 10 requests per day.
docs.endpoints.title: Endpoints
docs.chat.desc: Generate a model response for a conversation. This is the primary endpoint and is compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API.
docs.chat.param_model: The model ID to use (e.g. llama3.1:8b, mistral:7b, gemma2:9b). Use the /v1/models endpoint to list available models.
docs.chat.param_messages: An array of message objects. Each message has a role ("system", "user", or "assistant") and content (string).
docs.chat.param_temperature: Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. Lower values are more focused, higher values more creative. Defaults to 0.7.
docs.chat.param_max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate. Defaults to the model's maximum context length.
docs.chat.param_stream: If true, response is streamed using Server-Sent Events (SSE). See Streaming.
docs.models.desc: Returns a list of models currently available on the network, along with per-token pricing information.
docs.models.pricing_note: The routecat_pricing object is a RouteCat extension not present in the standard OpenAI response. It shows the current per-token cost in both USD and satoshis.
docs.register.desc: Create a new API key. No authentication required.
docs.register.param_name: A label for this key. Helps you identify it later.
docs.register.warn_body: Store your API key securely. It is only shown once at creation time and cannot be retrieved later.
docs.topup.desc: Generate a Lightning Network invoice to add satoshis to your account balance. Requires authentication.
docs.topup.param_amount: Amount in satoshis to add to your balance. Minimum: 100 sats.
docs.topup.note: Pay the returned Lightning invoice with any compatible wallet. Your balance updates automatically once the payment confirms.
docs.balance.desc: Check your current account balance and remaining free-tier requests. Requires authentication.
docs.balance.field_free: Free-tier requests remaining today (resets at midnight UTC)
docs.stats.desc: Public endpoint returning current gateway statistics. No authentication required.
docs.stats.field_nodes: Number of GPU provider nodes currently connected
docs.stats.field_jobs: Inference jobs completed in the last 24 hours
docs.stats.field_tokens: Total tokens generated in the last 24 hours
docs.stats.field_btc: Current BTC/USD exchange rate used for pricing
docs.stats.field_commit: Git commit hash of the running build
docs.audit.desc: Public audit log of recent inference jobs. Data is anonymized — no user-identifiable information is included. No authentication required.
docs.audit.note: The audit log provides transparency into how the network is being used. It is useful for providers monitoring demand and for anyone verifying the gateway's operation.
docs.int.title: Integrations
docs.int.desc: RouteCat works with any tool that supports the OpenAI API. Below you'll find step-by-step guides for the most popular ones. In every case, you only need two things:
docs.int.need_body:
API Key — get one for free at route.cat (Account section, "Create API Key")
Base URL — https://route.cat/v1
docs.int.models_note: To see which models are available right now, visit route.cat → Pricing or call GET /v1/models. Use the exact model name shown there (e.g. qwen2.5:7b, llama3.1:8b).
docs.int.vscode.title: VS Code — Continue
docs.int.vscode.desc: Continue is a free, open-source AI coding assistant for VS Code and JetBrains. It gives you autocomplete, chat, and inline editing — all powered by the model of your choice.
docs.int.vscode.s1d: Open VS Code, go to the Extensions panel (Ctrl+Shift+X), search Continue, and click Install.
docs.int.vscode.s2d: Click the Continue icon in the sidebar (the wand icon), then click the gear icon at the bottom of the Continue panel. This opens ~/.continue/config.yaml.
docs.int.vscode.s3d: Replace or add the following in your config.yaml:
docs.int.vscode.s4d: Open the Continue panel, select RouteCat from the model dropdown, and ask a question. That's it!
docs.int.vscode.tip_body: You can add multiple models — just repeat the block with a different model value. Check the available models at route.cat/pricing.
docs.int.cursor.title: Cursor
docs.int.cursor.desc: Cursor is an AI-first code editor built on VS Code. You can point it to RouteCat instead of the built-in models.
docs.int.cursor.s1d: Go to Cursor Settings (Ctrl+Shift+J or click the gear icon) → Models.
docs.int.cursor.s2d: Click + Add model, then choose OpenAI-compatible. Fill in:
docs.int.cursor.s3d: Select the new model from the model selector in the chat panel or inline edit. Start coding.
docs.int.openwebui.title: Open WebUI
docs.int.openwebui.desc: Open WebUI is a self-hosted web interface that looks and works like ChatGPT. You can connect it to RouteCat to chat with any available model through a familiar interface.
docs.int.openwebui.s1d: In Open WebUI, click your profile icon (bottom-left) → Settings → Connections.
docs.int.openwebui.s2d: Under OpenAI API, click + and fill in:
docs.int.openwebui.s3d: Click Save. The available models will appear in the model selector. Start a new chat, pick a model, and go.
docs.int.openwebui.tip_body: Install it with one command: docker run -d -p 3000:8080 ghcr.io/open-webui/open-webui:main. Then open localhost:3000.
docs.int.chatbox.title: ChatBox
docs.int.chatbox.desc: ChatBox is a free desktop app for Windows, Mac, and Linux. It's the easiest way to use RouteCat if you just want to chat — no terminal, no code.
docs.int.chatbox.s2d: Open Settings (gear icon) → AI Model Provider. Select OpenAI API Compatible and fill in:
docs.int.chatbox.s3d: Click Save and start a new conversation. Works exactly like ChatGPT.
docs.int.opencode.title: OpenCode (CLI)
docs.int.opencode.desc: OpenCode is a terminal-based AI coding assistant with a TUI interface. It supports custom providers, making it easy to use RouteCat for AI-assisted coding directly in your terminal.
docs.int.opencode.s2d: Store your RouteCat API key as an environment variable. Add this to your ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, or equivalent:
docs.int.opencode.s3d: Create an opencode.json at your project root (or globally at ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json):
docs.int.opencode.s4d: OpenCode will start in interactive mode. Ask it to edit files, explain code, or write tests — all powered by RouteCat.
docs.int.opencode.tip_body: The {env:ROUTECAT_API_KEY} syntax tells OpenCode to read the key from your environment — so your key never ends up committed to a repository. You can add more models to the models block at any time; check available ones at route.cat/pricing.
docs.int.generic.title: Any OpenAI-compatible app
docs.int.generic.desc: RouteCat works with any tool that supports the OpenAI API. If your app has a settings page for "OpenAI" or "Custom API", you can point it to RouteCat. Here's the universal pattern:
docs.int.generic.model_val: Any model from the pricing table (e.g. qwen2.5:7b)
docs.int.generic.env_desc: Many tools also accept configuration via environment variables:
docs.int.generic.warn_body: Always use the exact model name as shown in the pricing table, including the tag (e.g. qwen2.5:7b, not qwen2.5). If the model name doesn't match a connected node, you'll get a 503 error.
docs.streaming.title: Streaming
docs.streaming.desc: When stream: true is set in a chat completion request, the response is delivered as Server-Sent Events (SSE). Each event contains a chunk of the response, allowing you to display tokens as they are generated.
docs.streaming.format_desc: Each line is prefixed with data: followed by a JSON object. The stream ends with a data: [DONE] sentinel.
docs.billing.title: Billing
docs.billing.desc: RouteCat uses a simple per-token billing model. You pay only for what you use, with no monthly subscriptions or minimums.
docs.billing.step1: Top up your balance by paying a Lightning invoice (see /v1/auth/topup).
docs.billing.step2: Use tokens — each request deducts from your balance based on the model's per-token rate.
docs.billing.step3: Providers earn — 95% of the token cost goes to the GPU provider who served the request.
docs.billing.step4: Gateway fee — RouteCat takes a 5% fee to cover infrastructure and development.
docs.billing.pricing_desc: Each model has its own per-token rate, visible via the /v1/models endpoint. Prices are set in USD and converted to satoshis using the current BTC/USD exchange rate (updated every 5 minutes).
docs.billing.pricing_note: Prices shown in sats are approximate and depend on the current BTC/USD rate.
docs.billing.caps_desc: If your balance reaches zero during a request, the response will still complete — you are not cut off mid-generation. However, subsequent requests will return a 402 error until you top up. Free-tier users receive 10 requests per day regardless of balance.
docs.billing.conversion_body: The gateway fetches the BTC/USD rate every 5 minutes. The rate used for a given request is the rate in effect at the time the request is processed. You can check the current rate via /v1/stats.
docs.provider.title: Provider Guide
docs.provider.desc: Anyone with a GPU can earn Bitcoin by serving inference requests on the RouteCat network. Providers run Owlrun, a lightweight agent that connects to the gateway and processes jobs using locally-installed models via Ollama.
docs.provider.prereq1: A machine with a GPU (NVIDIA recommended, 8GB+ VRAM)
docs.provider.prereq2: A Lightning Network address for receiving payouts
docs.provider.step1_desc: Ollama provides the local model runtime. Install it and pull the models you want to serve.
docs.provider.step2_desc: Download the Owlrun binary for your platform from the releases page, or build from source.
docs.provider.step3_desc: Point Owlrun at the RouteCat gateway and provide your Lightning address for payouts.
docs.provider.earnings_desc: Providers earn 95% of the per-token cost for every request they serve. Earnings accumulate until they reach the payout threshold (1,000 sats by default), at which point the gateway automatically sends a Lightning payment to your configured address.
docs.provider.tip_body: Pull popular models to maximize your chances of being selected for jobs. Check /v1/stats to see current network demand.
docs.errors.title: Errors
docs.errors.desc: The API uses standard HTTP status codes. All error responses include a JSON body with an error field describing the issue.
docs.errors.400: Bad request. The request body is malformed or missing required fields.
docs.errors.401: Unauthorized. API key is missing, invalid, or revoked.
docs.errors.402: Payment required. Your balance is zero and free-tier quota is exhausted. Top up via /v1/auth/topup.
docs.errors.404: Not found. The requested model or endpoint does not exist.
docs.errors.429: Rate limited. You have exceeded 60 requests per minute. Retry after the Retry-After header interval.
docs.errors.500: Internal server error. Something went wrong on the gateway. Please try again or report the issue.
docs.errors.503: Service unavailable. No provider nodes are currently online for the requested model. Try again shortly or choose a different model.
docs.errors.retry_body: For 429 and 503 errors, implement exponential backoff with a maximum of 3 retries. For 500 errors, a single retry is usually sufficient.
docs.footer: RouteCat is open source. Contribute on GitHub.
community.title: Community
community.sub: Questions, ideas, or just want to say hi? Join the conversation.
community.channel.p: Announcements, updates, and new features. Follow to stay in the loop.
community.chat.p: Ask questions, report issues, share ideas, or help others. Open to everyone.
community.issues.p: Found a bug or have an improvement? Open an issue or submit a pull request on GitHub. Every contribution helps.
